description: Phase 4.2 boundary-snap config (see IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md). Pragmatic variable-length windows via Whisper sentence boundaries + pyannote silence gaps. CG-DETR deferred.
enabled_description: Master toggle. When false, Pass C's (clip_start, clip_end) values flow straight through unchanged.

sentence_snap.description: Snap start/end to the nearest word-level boundary in transcript.json. Uses Phase 3's WhisperX word-level timestamps when available; falls back to segment boundaries when they're not.
sentence_snap.max_end_drift_sec_description: End may drift LATER by up to 8 s to capture a payoff that lands just past the tentative window. Snapping earlier capped at 2 s — better to include extra than to chop the reaction.

silence_snap.description: After sentence-snapping, further nudge start/end to nearby silence gaps (gaps in the word-level timeline > silence_threshold_ms). Preserves clean cut points for Stage 7 render.

shot_cut_snap.description: TransNet V2 shot-boundary detection. Disabled by default — adds a ~50 MB model dep and ~30 s of inference per VOD. Enable only if your streams have frequent hard scene cuts (editing-heavy content, game menus, etc.).

duration_bounds.description: Absolute safety clamps on the snapped window. A snap that would exceed these is rejected and the original tentative boundaries are kept.

deferred.cg_detr: Saliency-guided moment retrieval via CG-DETR on ±90 s windows. Research-grade (needs QVHighlights-trained weights + SlowFast/CLIP features per window). Revisit after Phase 5 bootstraps a Twitch-clip eval dataset.
deferred.sg_detr_lighthouse: Same note — Lighthouse wraps CG-DETR/SG-DETR/QD-DETR but still needs training data that matches streaming content.
